meta.scope: Federal officials and gubernatorial candidates with documented contributions from CCP-linked donors (Pin Ni / Wanxiang Group, Dominic Ng / East West Bank, United Front Work Department-affiliated entities). Less mature than AIPAC tracking — seeded with high-confidence cases from FEC + investigative reporting at the Washington Examiner, Free Beacon, and Newsweek.
meta.descriptor: China-linked donor tracking. The CCP operates an explicit United Front Work Department to direct overseas members and proxies — including business executives such as Wanxiang Group's Pin Ni (named CCP member 15 times in Chinese state media) — to cultivate political relationships in the United States. Federal law prohibits CCP members from donating to U.S. campaigns, and most CCP-member donors hold green cards rather than citizenship, raising significant legal questions. RESOLUTE Citizen treats any documented contribution from a CCP-member or United Front-affiliated donor as a failure on america_first/q3.

candidates.name: Mikie Sherrill
candidates.note: Documented $14,500 to congressional campaign + $60,000 to super PAC + $5,800 maximum direct contribution from Pin Ni, a self-identified CCP member who leads the U.S. arm of Wanxiang Group (Hangzhou, China). Confirmed by FEC + Wanxiang's own 2025 annual report listing Pin as 'outstanding Communist Party member.' Pin is now also a major Sherrill gubernatorial donor.

candidates.name: Abigail Spanberger
candidates.note: $50,000 from Pin Ni (CCP member, Wanxiang) in two $25,000 installments April + May 2025, supporting Virginia gubernatorial bid. Pin is named in Chinese state media 15 times as 'outstanding Communist Party member.'

candidates.name: Cory Booker
candidates.note: Named beneficiary of Wanxiang Group's $1M+ federal-political-spending network. Specific Booker-direct amount conservatively bracketed at $10K pending FEC granular query. Pin has made 152 contributions totaling $1M+ since 2020, mostly to Democrats.

candidates.name: Tammy Duckworth
candidates.note: Named beneficiary of Wanxiang Group's federal-political-spending network. Specific amount conservatively bracketed at $10K pending granular FEC query.

candidates.name: Steve Daines
candidates.note: Named beneficiary of Wanxiang Group's federal-political-spending network — one of the few Republican recipients identified. Conservatively bracketed at $5K pending granular FEC query.

candidates.name: Ro Khanna
candidates.donor: Dominic Ng (East West Bank CEO, China-linked)
candidates.note: $5,800 from East West Bank CEO Dominic Ng between March 2021 and May 2022. Khanna was subsequently elevated to ranking member of the House Select Committee on the CCP, raising direct conflict-of-interest concerns. Ng has well-documented Chinese-state-aligned business interests.

candidates.name: Raja Krishnamoorthi
candidates.note: Took multiple donations from China-linked donors during his tenure as top Democrat on the House Select Committee on the CCP. Conservatively bracketed pending granular FEC tally.

candidates.name: Chuck Schumer
candidates.donor: United Front Work Department-affiliated NY donors
candidates.note: Identified by Newsweek investigation as one of the major NY-state recipients of the $1M+ donor network with documented ties to the United Front Work Department of the CCP. Conservatively bracketed pending granular tally.

verified_zero.name: Thomas Massie
verified_zero.note: No documented donations from CCP-linked donors or United Front Work Department affiliates in any FEC-searchable cycle. Massie is the most consistent congressional skeptic of foreign-influence campaign money.

verified_zero.name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
verified_zero.note: No documented donations from CCP-linked donors or United Front Work Department affiliates in any FEC-searchable cycle. Greene has consistently voted against U.S. funding for the WHO and other China-affiliated multilateral bodies.

verified_zero.name: James Fishback
verified_zero.note: Candidate-as-self-funder; no documented donations from CCP-linked donors or United Front Work Department affiliates in any FEC-searchable cycle. Fishback's Azoria capital fund has publicly committed to no-China-exposure investing.
